Hey guys JB is in the house. What's up? A lot of really interesting things to talk to you about here But I think will be incredibly helpful. Scripts in the real world because I say this in the training many times But I think I think I have a better way now of explain to you, you know the same guy who who had this company said to me the next because you you gotta Figure out a different name for for your script writing because what you do is you don't use scripts It's like dynamic presenting. I was like, damn. That's actually pretty smart. That's what he realized is that the fluidity That I applied to a written script It's perfectly written that the script is actually Changing as I use it in each presentation But the way it's structured Because each word is perfectly written I have these anchor points that I can always come back to in other words What I said to them is this guys we're gonna write a pattern There's two things that you must know by heart You have to know how the pattern begins the first five or six words memorized and The last five or six words out you have to know the beginning and the end of each chunk in your script Why because in the real world of sales what you're gonna find is you're moving all these patterns around the board Depending on your interactions with the customer and what happens is is that if you know Your landing if you have an automated landing meaning the last five or eight words of the pattern That's your landing where you're landing and you have an automated takeoff being you know those five or six words in the beginning by heart Then the sort of the stuff in between the two pieces of bread the sandwich We've got the actual pattern itself while you definitely want to write that out perfectly words for word in perfectly spoken English That is gonna somewhat change in all three because what's gonna happen is the context Changes based on what the prospect said to you what I kept doing with them We had this amazing role-playing exercises and they watched me do like my Jedi trick here Which which I think at first they were like how the hell are you doing that? I think they realized by that if I date you like oh my god I see how he does it like it sounds so good what I do But when I broke it down for them and slowed it down They start to realize what I was doing which was I have the safety Safety sandwiches where I know the beginning of a pattern and the end of a pattern and I could lump whatever I want in the middle And if I get lost I'll use an essence act I have my little store words in the middle, but since I always know where I'm going as I'm doing the end of my pattern guess what? Because I'm using my unconscious mind. It's memorized right it's completely memorized my conscious mind is Read up to say where should I go next? What's the next thing I should say? What's my next pattern and whichever pad I choose what happens guess what I? Have the beginning of that one memorized I Can hook it's like imagine a bunch of trains and they all these box cars right and every train's got different Contents inside each car take a long train right inside the train one cars got oats and honey Another cars got metal bricks or red bricks or a red right, but the end of the car They all link up to each other because they're forming each their structure the same at the beginning in the end So you can build the train as long as you want a train could be a mile long and have all different contents inside That was what one of the really came out and and because of that it allows me to always sound elegant I'm all I can listen to the prospect and That along with that the full day almost on intelligence gathering Well, I started showing them how you really use the straight line to gather intelligence where how Based on what the prospect actually says to you is gonna have a huge Impact on how you respond yet somehow every sales the same why because see that's what and this is That's what these healthy Valendries are all about in other words. I Think this is this idea here watch so this is your open This is your clothes right when you're directly on the line This is like when you're reading the exact words from your script everything is perfect, right? Then you have these valendries above and below So I say when you're directly on the line You're doing the talking and your prospect is listening I say when you're off the straight line, but inside these boundaries your Prospect is doing the talking and you're doing the listening and that's true, too, but Also, there's this other zone. I really should divide this buffer into these two areas here It's like almost two more divisions and I might do this in future trades I just realized just myself is that almost to break it down that this is where you're talking but free-forming In your script, in other words if they're talking here You are going to change your core script But not just just enough to have context so it makes sense See if you if you really try This is make sense to you. I think it does Travis makes sense to you Travis is not gonna say anything. I said, yeah, sure. I make sense My paycheck I love Travis is awesome. Right anyway. No, the point is is that There's this sort of wiggle room that every salesman needs to have and it's healthy That you're using your script But you're being a human You can't possibly map out Every interaction based on okay, if you're if you say so, how long you're living in the area for There's an infinite number way of ways the prospect can respond. That's your question, right? I can't give you based on every answer Flow this way that way three levels down you have 5,000 combinations You get it, but your brain your unconscious and conscious mind working in tandem Can easily manage that process As long as you know what questions you need to ultimately ask What main questions you need to ask and When you're in your main presentation Beginning of each pan the end of each pan and the best way to say certain things like the best way to Explain the company the product the idea the best way to sell yourself the best way to sell the company that stands behind the product So there's the product the company stands behind the product you three tense right in other words The point is there is this wiggle room here Right very tight on the straight line That represents actual human communication that can't be scripted That shouldn't be scripted that shouldn't be scripted But what happens is you have to have it written as if it's scripted because that serves as your roadmap It's your roadmap from point a to point b which is from open to close There's gonna be many ziggies and sags within that map But what you don't want to do is go off wildly off the Pluto. I'll give you an example Here was a perfect practical example of going off to Pluto a woman there very smart young lady Okay, who was doing a role-play that was miss the ocean's egg There's two people do it a role-play right and I love them both. They were great, right? And and she was and I said so um it was the subject was financial Qualification, how do you know if someone's qualified? How do you get that information out of them? And they were you know having strung with it right and I came up with a pattern, you know that was you know listen So you'll see oh so tell me you know tell me what your relationship with success been like and money now You could never say that as a card salesman you say to someone kind of what your relationship of money like they'll say What is wrong with you? But as someone selling coaching services and certifications for that that's a very rational question because they're going to be a coach They want to be a speaker so tell me what your relationship with success and money been like in the past And then you say in terms of have you struggled with it, right? That's a good opening question, right? So that's one way to go about it but In the real world of qualifying What quickly came up as they pressed me? Because it was a very important issue to them is like when do you ask that when do you not ask that? I'm like well, let me explain to you the overarching Parameter here Is that you never want to ask any question just for the sake of asking the question? Either there's a reason to ask it Something you'll benefit or they'll benefit by or you don't ask it And by the way, if let's just say you need you need to know I need to know If they're qualified that's like a safety check in the back of my head before I go into my main presentation Okay, I need to know if they're financially qualified. Okay, and if they're interested although Not interested still a level of interest. I couldn't help myself. I had to say it. All right. Just disregard us Wish I got it. All right. There's a sweet spot There's a sweet spot Although when I ask grand chrome, what's the reason there is no sweet spot Anyone want to talk about sweet spots, right? There's a sweet spot here and the sweet spot is basically that you want to Have them aware of what Not taking action has been costing them is costing them right now Most of the boy cost in the future But if you can do that and make them feel really good about you in the process Wow, then you've taken things to a whole new level and I'll tell you what as I showed them When we kept playing we kept roping and I kept being the salesperson I showed them what I do and by the end of day one They were like and by day two they're like, oh my god I'd like that, you know And I'm like, you know guys, I know you think that it makes me seem like I'm an invincibly great salesperson But you know what can I be honest with you? It's just I'm just I've been doing it for so long And I have it so perfected, but it's not a magic trick guys and it's certainly not reserved just for me I'm not as I'm a bright guy, but that brightness is not what allows me to close Now it's part of what makes me a natural born closer But I'm telling you that once you understand what I do Which is the straight line system by the way, you'll very quickly realize that every sale is exactly the same And that was the big takeaway from this weekend and by the time we were Completed we really just you know, we just had this amazing sort of you know, you know a group understanding of what they needed to do to close What they need to do to get people to really open up to them to feel good about it How to explain their product, you know one of the other things was like, you know, how do you you know, John Maxwell? Yeah, it's too good to be true But then again, this is John Maxwell and of course John Maxwell is a servant based leader He's not a capitalist pig like me. No, I'm just kidding. He's not like, you know, he's not a pure capitalist John Maxwell is someone that goes out there as money was not one of his highest values ever He's got a very pure pristine message and vision that he loves to deliver And he leveraged that message by hiring other people as coaches But for him it was never really a money seriously just was not he was already very wealthy He's written a hundred books, you know, number one or I'm I'm selling this company to you now And now it's after the John Maxwell program, right? No, but the point is I'm just making a very important distinction here is that is that John has so much credibility And poor Martinelli who structured the program just did this massive over deliver thing that when people Hear it for the first time. They're like Hey, I'm overwhelmed because there's too much. It's too good. And how could that be possible for only this amount of money? Right. So what do you do with that? Well, and I've been through this many times before because it's not the only company that does this right companies Well run companies very ethical companies. They try to overlook what I do. You guys know I could have charged more of this So it's not a unique situation, but it's just that's their situation at a very extreme level They have all this stuff that they offer the customer the new the new coach is like It's just it's it's not quite you're like, well, I don't stand it's only that I never pay again You think there'll be an annual renewal fee, right? But there's not and there's no trick up this leave. It's just that's what they did, right? but unless you explain that to the prospect That's going to be a festering thought in their mind not everyone but a lot of them are going to be thinking You know what? Wow, it sounds great, but it just sounds too good to be true And usually with that objection, they don't even say it It's not one that gets that's one of those classic objections. They say, oh, wow It sounds really good. Let me think about it when they're really saying is it sounds too good to be true Sounds too good to be true because what they haven't done yet is they haven't connected the dots that I connected for you In other words, I started off by framing for you. I run the mother to research what I said that for a reason All right, so this whole his whole mission in life is not about how much money Can I make how long how big is my yacht? How many homes do I own? Am I on the Forbes 400? His mission is primary values giving value right so You have to do this. So that's part of the equation what you want to do is build this into your presentation Not so I know you're thinking it's too good to be true but by Seeding this you want to connect the dots. I see if this is an issue for you in your main presentation You want to be connecting up those dots for people Don't make them come to the to the own conclusion two or three loops later or maybe two days later after I do some research If you have a situation where you have a very a fine reputation for the company and you've structured an offer That's just so ridiculously good And you're saying why people are they seem skeptical still because they are thinking it's too good to be true Now I wouldn't want you to go out saying I know you're thinking it's too good to be true But it's not although wouldn't be the worst thing in the world You want to dance around that in your framing of your pitch? Anyway, so the point is So the point is is that You have to when you're writing your presentation What's the first thing you do before you start? You say, yeah, I'm gonna put myself in my prospect shoes What are they going to be thinking? What is the reality of what I sell of what I offer? What are their preconceived notions going to be when they walk in my door or pick up a telephone or click on my website? Well, click on my website. What are their preconceived notions? And if it's clear in this case crystal clear that any person that knew anything about business or coaching Or even just life in general, they would hear the office and say There's something wrong there. I get constant coaching and constant written Yeah But if you say it after the fact you're almost saying it on defense When you write a great script like a world-class presentation, you're using offense You're building that answer the antidote to that warrior thesis As an underlying thesis to your entire presentation that runs concurrent with everything else you do Make sense I think I'm going to get that guys On top of that what else we discovered this two days is always great Discussion right is is like my way of teaching is what I discovered like I I took this playing, you know guys Just you understand as I talk to you And I'm playing the role of the salesperson for John Maxwell I have these like things in the back of my head like overarching. I cannot break the pool I must remember energy and benefits out. I have these things. I'm remembering here, right? They're just overarching. That's what I mean by overarching equations So I'm always aware in the back of my mind that I can't violate those overarching principles So when I'm running my patterns and doing what I do, I'm always obeying those principles So when you're writing your scripts your language friends remember It's not just like it's not a logic base. I want to do a quick kind of You know, I want to give you some food authority and then we'll go into our Q&A here and hopefully have a lot of questions All right, and I can answer your questions. I have a makeup Q&A for you sometime next week. All right So let me just give you my quick, you know daily dose of standards here what I what I want for you. Okay I think that the problem That most of us have all of us including me Is that when we consider the inner game of success Our vision for the future our ability to manage our state Our ability to focus on that vision All personal standards Okay, and of course what our belief systems, right when we consider all that stuff I think we forget that they all wrap up And cross over into each other you can't have standards that don't Cross over and are supported or not supported by your beliefs See if your beliefs run counter to your standards They're not going to be standards very long It will drop your standards to match your beliefs vice versa if you're Not out there with a vision for your fusion you're floundering. Guess what? After a while you're starting to develop limiting beliefs Because you don't know where you're going you'll have trouble succeeding those days. They're all Crossing over into one another Why am I saying this to you because in the certification training you'll notice that I Made very specifically this point that is going to be these four distinct inner-world modules I'm going to ask you right now that when you're done We're going to be you know those who win the first wave of this training here right as many of you right We're going to be we have a completion date of september 15th right around there, right It's a completion date and then you know you'll get it that date around 13th to 14th And then you'll finish that last module over those four or five days, right? But once you are done once you've made your first pass It's not your last pass Part of having high standards, right is this Striving for personal perfection Now perfection is not when I say perfection. I don't mean it's like it's impossible to achieve perfection But you strive for it.
